x-enumeration-literals.name: Individual Funeral Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Covers a single person (the policyholder or insured). The benefit is paid to the nominated beneficiary upon the insured’s death.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Family Funeral Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Covers the policyholder and immediate family members (spouse, children). Premiums are based on age and number of dependents.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Extended Family Funeral Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Expands coverage to include extended relatives such as parents, siblings, in-laws, or grandparents — common in many African markets.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Group Funeral Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Covers members of an organization, company, or association (e.g., employees, church members, unions) under a single master policy.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Assistance / Service-Based Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Instead of a cash payout, the insurer provides funeral services directly (coffin, transport, ceremony arrangements, etc.) through partner providers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cash Benefit (Lump Sum) Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Pays out a fixed lump sum amount to the beneficiary for them to manage funeral expenses themselves.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Employer Funeral Benefit Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Provided by employers as part of employee benefits, covering the employee (and sometimes family members) for funeral-related expenses.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Micro Funeral Policy
x-enumeration-literals.description: Low-cost version of funeral insurance targeting low-income individuals; offers limited cover with simplified underwriting.
